title: Grammars of the Living World
subtitle: Body, Story, Culture
author: PlayfulProcess
thesis: We cannot know reality directly. What we can know is that bodies in parasympathetic states relate better, that relating well builds adaptive cultures, and that humans have always needed shared symbolic structures — grammars — to give the intellect something to do with this opening and to scaffold the relationships it makes possible.

parts.title: THE BODY
parts.epigraph: The reader enters through the body, not the wound.

parts.title: THE CRISIS
parts.epigraph: What happens when the hypothesis is violated.

parts.title: THE GRAMMARS
parts.epigraph: Humanity's oldest response to the crisis.

parts.title: THE HINGE
parts.epigraph: One chapter that changes the terms.

parts.title: THE GESTURE
parts.epigraph: Not a solution but a practice.
